title: Accessibility flags catalog
description: The four boolean flags on Theme.accessibility_flags jsonb + user_theme_preferences.accessibility_overrides. Each flag maps to a specific CSS media-query augmentation applied at compile time by the CssRenderer binding.

flags.description: Increase contrast between color.text.* and color.surface.* pairs to WCAG AAA (7:1 body / 4.5:1 large text). Also thickens outlines + focus rings for visibility at low vision.
flags.notes: Also honored via OS-level prefers-contrast: more. When the user's preference AND the OS both indicate high contrast, the compiled CSS auto-picks the high-contrast preset variant.

flags.description: Zero all motion.duration.* tokens so animations become instant. Users with vestibular disorders + motion sickness benefit.
css_overrides_applied: Additional @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block adds animation: none !important + transition: none !important

flags.description: Scale typography.*.size.* by +25% for improved readability at low vision. Also honors iOS Dynamic Type + Android font scaling implicitly via rem-based sizing.
flags.notes: Independent of OS text-scale — the flag applies +25% on TOP of whatever OS scaling the user has set. Users on iOS with 130% Dynamic Type AND this flag see effective 162.5% scale.

flags.description: Force :focus outline on every interactive element regardless of pointer-vs-keyboard interaction. Compliance with EN 301 549 + WCAG 2.4.7.
flags.notes: For keyboard-only users the default @focus-visible behavior already surfaces the ring. This flag forces the ring for pointer users too — useful for cognitive-disability users who need visual anchors.

seeder_contract: The 4 flags are seeded as constants — not persisted in a table.
Theme.accessibility_flags always carries all 4 keys with boolean values (default all false). Validated by ThemeObserver via valid_accessibility_flags_shape rule.
user_theme_preferences.accessibility_overrides may carry a subset of the 4 keys (only the ones the user explicitly overrode). Missing keys fall through to the tenant's default.
